answer:
**Several works in the Indic *Dharmaśāstra* tradition discuss the relationship between rulers (kings) and religious classes (primarily Brahmins as priests, advisors, and court officials). These texts prescribe kings appointing Brahmin priests (*purohita*), consulting them in justice and governance, and relying on them for legitimacy and counsel, often framing the king as protector of *varṇas* with Brahmins at the apex.[^1][^2][^3]**

- **Kātyāyana Smṛti**: Chapter 2 details the king's duties, emphasizing appointment of a qualified Brahmin priest (*purohita*) free from greed.[^4]
- **Bṛhaspati Smṛti**: Describes the king examining disputes with ministers and a family priest (*purohita*), alongside assessors.[^5]
- **Manusmṛti**: Chapter 7 outlines the king's origin from divine particles, his duty to serve aged Brahmins learned in the Vedas for counsel, and protection of *varṇas* with Brahmin priority.[^6]

**Western works like Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation* (Vol. I, ch. 8) critique historical clerical influence on rulers and laws, viewing clergy as rivals to civil power.[^7] No other works (e.g., poetry or elegies) cover this topic institutionally.**

[^1]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, ch. 2, [/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/02/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/02/translation/).

[^2]: Bṛhaspati Smṛti, ch. 1, [/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/).

[^3]: Manusmṛti, ch. 7, [/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/).

[^4]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, ch. 2, [/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/02/translation/#p-ba5f1a](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/02/translation/#p-ba5f1a).

[^5]: Bṛhaspati Smṛti, ch. 1, [/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-e91140](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-e91140).

[^6]: Manusmṛti, ch. 7, [/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-cbe0d7](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-cbe0d7).

[^7]: *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, ch. 8, [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/08-du-systeme-qui-fait-d-une-religion-positive-le-fon/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/08-du-systeme-qui-fait-d-une-religion-positive-le-fon/translation/).
